narrative:
The Summit Labs climb opened quietly: an application sent on a Tuesday, a referral from Jamie doing the early carrying. The screen came first, and the payments-outage story did what it always does — turned a get-to-know-you call into a conversation about judgement under pressure.

The technical round was the steep pitch. The debrief afterwards was honest about it: the systems depth landed, but the on-call ownership question deserved a sharper answer, and the next stage got that answer because the last one taught it.

Forty-one days after base camp, the offer arrived. Not a lucky summit — a climbed one: every stage fed the next, and the story the interviewers heard by the end was the one worth telling.
